title: carelia arbutifolia
intro: Carelia arbutifolia, also known as the manchineel tree, is a highly toxic plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and edible fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carelia arbutifolia include treating skin conditions, wounds, and reducing fever, as well as being used as a natural remedy for digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Carelia arbutifolia are often made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, but caution must be exercised due to its highly toxic nature.
